title: The Balcony

'''THE BALCONY'''
= {{Underline|ffffff50}}{{User:JakitoFan/Class Custom/4|class=PSI|hexagon=Ψ|header=THREAT INDEX - CLASS|safety=Unsafe, Unstable|security=Insecure|entity=Severe Psychological and Radiological Hazards|bg-image=Black-Screen.png|bg-size=5000px|text-color=#ffffff|chevron=>}}
''The Balcony is an Enigmatic Level within the Backrooms.''
==
'''DESCRIPTION'''
== {{Underline|ffffff50}}
{{Blurimage|width=250px|file=Balcony rewrite?.png|hovertext=An illusion.|caption=A rare image of The Balcony.}}
The Balcony manifests primarily as a network of exceedingly small Euclidean hotel rooms, each equipped with a balcony. The entire level is believed to be infinite in length and height, but only roughly 100 meters in width. The facade and most interior surfaces are entirely comprised of concrete or similar materials. Each balcony is uniform in structure and bare in its contents.

The rooms here are equally uniform and mostly spartan in nature, comprising only of a mattress on the floor with a thin blanket and rug, a bare closet sheltered by wooden doors, and a single wooden table with no chairs. There is a small, dirtied bathroom in the corner of each room, a set of glass doors to the infinite array of balconies and a 'front door' to an infinitely long hallway connecting the interior of the gigantic building to the exterior. The hallway is constructed out of concrete, excluding shattered marble tiles that occasionally dot the walls. These hallways may rarely have metal staircases leading to different hallways on separate floors of the building.

==
'''ENTITY'''
== {{Underline|ffffff50}}
{{Blurimage|width=250px|file=Balconyimage2.png|caption=A second image of The Balcony.|hovertext=Darkness.}}
The sole species of entity in The Balcony are known as Illusions. Illusions manifest as indistinct, shadowy forms that at first glance resemble other common entities such as [[Entity 3|smilers]] or [[Entity 9|facelings]], although with a darker color scheme. The closer one gets to an Illusion, the darker it appears, until it eventually turns entirely pitch black. If one gets too close to an Illusion, it will spontaneously disappear, replaced by a small purest-black sphere. This sphere emits extreme amounts of both radiation and [[Phenomenon 35|psychic energy]], causing a very high fatality risk if wanderers do not vacate the area immediately. Even if one escapes, they will need emergency treatment, and fatalities are still common. Illusions are entirely stationary, allowing wanderers to avoid them as long as they simply keep their distance. Illusions appear exclusively on the balconies of the level.

Excluding Illusions, no entities are present. ==
'''PHENOMENA'''
== {{Underline|ffffff50}} A notable phenomenon affecting the level is that of [[Phenomenon 15]], preventing wanderers from meeting each other in the level. However, this could also be due to the gargantuan and presumably infinite size of the level, and its difficulty to enter. The phenomenon of [[Level 483|Level Grayscale]] seems to affect the level as well, as the entire level is monochromatic and grayscale. It is believed that [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]] did not affect The Balcony in any meaningful way, because records from prior to The Flicker report identical appearances and details. The level seems to prevent any form of no-clipping out, except in certain manners. ==
'''ENTRANCES AND EXITS'''
== {{Underline|ffffff50}} ==
'''ENTRANCES'''
== {{Underline|ffffff25}} *The sole method of entering The Balcony is noclipping in exceedingly rare second-floor balconies amongst the [[Level 164|Structures in the Dark]]. ==
'''EXITS'''
== {{Underline|ffffff25}} *A rare hotel room emitting Victorian music may appear within the level, locating it and entering will send you to [[Level 406|a Victorian household, fitting for its melody]]. *Taking the decision to jump off a balcony and fall into the darkness below will lead to [[The Void]], [[Level 188.65|The Hyperbolic Hotel]], [[The Call|𝖆 𝖍𝖔𝖚𝖘𝖊 𝖜𝖎𝖙𝖍 𝖆 𝖗𝖎𝖓𝖌𝖎𝖓𝖌 𝖕𝖍𝖔𝖓𝖊]], or the now-familiar hum-buzz of [[Level 0|The Lobby]].

??? - Uhh, I think I entered a new level by accident.

Agent Smith - Alright, sir. Stand by while I fetch the right documents.

Agent Smith - *Ambient sounds of paper ruffling*.

Agent Smith - Alright, first question. How did you enter this level?

??? - I was relaxing on the top of a rooftop in [[Level 164|164]], but then I tripped and would have hit face first into a balcony before I no-clipped here.

Agent Smith - Okay, second question. What does the level look like?

??? - From what I've seen, it's just a shit ton of hotel rooms with balconies looking out into the dark.

Agent Smith - Alright, sir. Next question, have you seen any entities?

??? - Yes, I have. There's this shadowy guy on the balcony, kind of indistinct. I'm gonna go see what's up.

Agent Smith - Sir, please do not approach the entity.

Agent Smith - Sir?

??? - *Sounds of a glass door opening and then extreme interference.*

??? - It's go[ne?], there's just [a bl]ack sph[ere] he[re].

Agent Smith - Sir, I can barely hear you.

??? - ...

??? - *thud.*

??? - ...

[CONNECTION LOST, CALL HAS BEEN DISCONNECTED.]
